items.headline: Google Releases Gemma 4 Under Apache 2.0 — Biggest Open Model Move Yet
items.summary: Google dropped Gemma 4 in four sizes (2B to 31B), all under Apache 2.0 — a license change that may matter more than benchmarks. Runs on phones, supports 140+ languages, multimodal, and built specifically for agentic workflows. Architecture draws from Gemini 3 research. NVIDIA already shipping optimized RTX versions. Massive for enterprise and local AI.

items.headline: Microsoft Launches 3 In-House AI Models, Suleyman Targets Frontier Models by 2027
items.summary: Microsoft's AI Superintelligence team (led by Mustafa Suleyman) debuted three proprietary models covering speech transcription, voice synthesis, and image generation — priced below Google and Amazon equivalents. Post-reorg, Suleyman is now focused entirely on frontier research and superintelligence. Microsoft is quietly reducing OpenAI dependency while maintaining the partnership.

items.headline: Anthropic Secretly Building 'Conway' — An Always-On Persistent Claude Agent
items.summary: Reports emerged that Anthropic is developing 'Conway,' an always-on Claude agent with persistent memory and real-time responsiveness — a significant shift toward continuous agentic AI. No public announcement yet but described as one of Anthropic's most important internal projects.

items.headline: SpaceX Confidentially Files for $75B IPO at $1.75 Trillion Valuation
items.summary: SpaceX filed confidentially for what could be the largest IPO on record, targeting $75B raised at a $1.75T valuation. Follows February's $250B merger with Elon Musk's xAI. Plans to use funds to expand AI data centers in space. OpenAI and Anthropic IPOs expected to follow.

items.headline: US Venture Funding Hits Record $267B in Q1 2026 — OpenAI, Anthropic, xAI Dominate
items.summary: PitchBook data shows Q1 2026 VC funding smashed all records at $267B, driven almost entirely by AI mega-rounds. Exit value hit $347.3B — also a record — largely driven by SpaceX's $250B xAI acquisition. AI investment now accounts for the majority of US VC activity.

items.headline: OpenAI DoD Deployment Triggers #QuitGPT Movement — 2.5M Supporters, Installs Down 295%
items.summary: OpenAI's agreement to deploy AI on US Department of Defense classified networks sparked massive public backlash. The #QuitGPT movement attracted over 2.5 million supporters and ChatGPT uninstalls surged 295% overnight. Anthropic and Google are watching closely.

items.headline: OpenAI Surpasses $25B Annualized Revenue, Eyes Late-2026 IPO
items.summary: OpenAI has crossed $25B in annualized revenue and is reportedly taking early steps toward a public listing, potentially as soon as late 2026. The company raised $122B in its most recent round at an $852B valuation. Meanwhile, OpenAI secondary market demand is softening as Anthropic heats up with institutional investors.

items.headline: Alibaba Qwen 3.6 Plus Free on OpenRouter — Agentic Coding Focus
items.summary: Alibaba launched Qwen 3.6 Plus (available free on OpenRouter), specifically targeting agentic coding workflows. Released March 30. As Chinese labs pull back from fully open releases on latest models, it remains accessible but with more enterprise guardrails.

items.headline: Vermont Signs First State AI Election Law — Synthetic Media in Campaigns Now Regulated
items.summary: Vermont's S.23 — regulating AI-generated synthetic media in election campaigns — was signed into law after passing both chambers. The bill, in progress for 14 months, is among the first state-level laws directly targeting AI use in political advertising. More states are expected to follow.

items.headline: AI Crisis Contractor ThroughLine Eyes Expansion into Violent Extremism Prevention
items.summary: ThroughLine, the NZ-based startup hired by OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google to redirect at-risk users to crisis helplines, is expanding beyond self-harm and domestic violence into violent extremism prevention. Comes after a Canadian school shooting suspect was banned from ChatGPT without authorities being notified.

items.headline: Markets Close for Good Friday — Week Ends with First Rally Since Iran War Started
items.summary: US markets are closed today for Good Friday. Thursday's session capped the first winning week since the Iran war began: Nasdaq +4.4%, S&P 500 +3.4%, Dow +3.0% week-to-date. Thursday saw slim gains as markets overcame early losses. S&P 500 closed at ~6,582. NYSE and Nasdaq reopen Monday April 6.

items.headline: Brent Crude Hits $141/Barrel — Highest Since 2008 Financial Crisis
items.summary: Oil prices surged to $141/barrel (Brent), the highest since the 2008 Financial Crisis, driven by Iran war supply disruptions and Hormuz Strait uncertainty. Started at ~$80-82 when war began Feb 28, now up ~75%. IEA warns supply crunch will worsen through April and is weighing additional strategic reserve releases.

items.headline: Iran War Fuel Crisis: ~20% of Global LNG Offline, Europe Faces Second Energy Shock in 4 Years
items.summary: Two Qatar Energy Ras Laffan liquefaction trains (~3.5% of global capacity) were damaged by Iranian missile strikes and may be offline for years. Europe is now confronting its second major energy shock since 2022. California faces sky-high gas prices due to tight Asian fuel supply. Economists raising 2026 stagflation risk forecasts.

items.headline: SpaceX $75B IPO Filing — Could Be Largest on Record at $1.75T Valuation
items.summary: SpaceX's confidential IPO filing (April 1) targets a $1.75T valuation and $75B raise — potentially largest IPO in history. SpaceX merged with xAI in February for $250B. Plans include AI data centers in space. Microsoft's OpenAI stake could generate $150B+ in non-operating income mark-to-market gains.

items.headline: Jefferies Warns Markets Underpricing West Asia Risks — Oil Shock Could Turn 'Systemic'
items.summary: Jefferies analysts warned of a striking disconnect between geopolitical realities and market behavior. Only 28% of S&P 500 stocks are above their 50-day moving average — up from sub-20% at recent lows but far from healthy. Jefferies says a true oil demand destruction event could cascade into systemic risk.

items.headline: Nike Hits 11-Year Low After Mixed Q3 Earnings — Stock Down 15%
items.summary: Nike stock fell 15% after reporting mixed Q3 results with weak forward guidance, hitting an 11-year low. CEO Elliott Hill insists the turnaround plan is working but analysts aren't buying it. The stock is down significantly YTD and over the past year vs the market.

items.headline: RH (Restoration Hardware) Plunges ~20% After Growth Wobbles
items.summary: RH shares cratered roughly 20% after the luxury home furnishings retailer reported growth concerns. Reported the same day as Nike's miss, making for an ugly earnings session before the holiday weekend.

items.headline: Global M&A Hits Record $1.2 Trillion in Q1 2026 Despite Geopolitical Headwinds
items.summary: Global mergers and acquisitions hit a record $1.2T in Q1 2026, driven almost entirely by AI-related deals. SpaceX-xAI ($250B) is the headline transaction. Private markets are under pressure from a retail investor push and war-related volatility but deal flow remains strong in AI infrastructure.

items.headline: Oracle Lays Off 30,000 Amid Restructuring
items.summary: Oracle announced a massive 30,000-person layoff as the company restructures around cloud and AI workloads. One of the largest tech layoffs of the year.

items.headline: US Job Growth Slowing to 'Virtual Standstill' as Iran War Hits Economy
items.summary: Reuters analysis warns that US job growth is decelerating sharply, describing it as approaching a 'virtual standstill.' The Iran war's energy shock and uncertainty are now visibly filtering through to employment. Economists are increasingly citing stagflation risk for 2026.

items.headline: Iran War Day 33: Trump Strikes Tehran Bridge, Destroys Medical Center, Warns 'Haven't Even Started'
items.summary: US-Israeli strikes escalated sharply on Day 33 of the Iran war. Trump celebrated a strike on a major Tehran highway bridge and a leading public health institution was destroyed. Iranian President Pezeshkian called attacks on medical facilities 'a crime against humanity' and urged WHO response. Trump warned on social media that US military 'hasn't even started.' Students killed in Fars Province.

items.headline: UK Deploys Rapid Sentry Air Defense to Kuwait; France Crosses Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: The UK announced it is sending the Rapid Sentry air defense system to Kuwait as Iran continues attacking Gulf infrastructure. A French naval vessel crossed the Strait of Hormuz — the first Western warship to do so since the war began. Dozens of countries are seeking ways to restart energy shipments through Hormuz.

items.headline: Europe's Second Energy Crisis: IEA Considers Strategic Reserve Releases as Gas Supply Collapses
items.summary: The Iran war has triggered what analysts describe as the world's largest supply disruption since the 1970s energy crisis. Europe faces fuel shortages, sky-high prices, and fertilizer shortages threatening food security. IEA is weighing emergency strategic reserve releases. Economists have raised 2026 inflation and stagflation projections.

items.headline: Iran Attacks Kuwait Desalination Plant; Gulf States Scrambling for Water Security
items.summary: Iran struck a Kuwait desalination plant — one of several attacks on civilian infrastructure across the Gulf. Trump had earlier threatened to destroy Iran's own desalination plants in retaliation. Regional water security is now a growing concern as the war enters its second month.

items.headline: Hungary Intelligence Spied on Opposition Tisza Party Ahead of April Election
items.summary: Hungary's intelligence service is reported to have spied on the opposition Tisza Party ahead of the country's parliamentary election scheduled for April 2026, raising serious concerns about democratic integrity in the EU member state.

items.headline: Trump Fires AG Pam Bondi Over Epstein Files Frustration
items.summary: President Trump ousted Attorney General Pam Bondi on Thursday, framing it as a move to the private sector. Multiple reports cite Trump's frustration with her handling of the Jeffrey Epstein files release and her limited success pursuing his political rivals. Bondi is the second attorney general fired by Trump.

items.headline: Iran War Exposing Cracks in 'No-Hire' US Economy — Job Growth Near Standstill
items.summary: Economists are pointing to the Iran war as an accelerant on a pre-existing slowdown. US job growth was already decelerating before the conflict; the energy shock is now threatening to tip the labor market into contraction. Energy sector jobs are booming but broader private sector hiring has stalled.

items.headline: Tiger Woods Seeks Treatment After DUI Arrest, Misses Masters for Second Straight Year
items.summary: Tiger Woods announced he is stepping away to seek treatment four days after being arrested on suspicion of DUI in Florida. He will miss the Masters for the second consecutive year. The announcement came Tuesday; his vehicle had crashed in Florida.

items.headline: US Stock Markets Closed for Good Friday — Reopen Monday April 6
items.summary: NYSE and Nasdaq are closed today for Good Friday. The US bond market closes at noon ET. Markets reopen Monday April 6. Today is also Easter weekend (Easter Sunday April 5), making this a holiday-shortened week for financial markets.

items.headline: Runner Led Off Course at Atlanta Half Marathon Gets Team USA World Championship Spot
items.summary: Jess McClain was the likely winner of the Atlanta Half Marathon before being directed off course. After the incident, she has been awarded a spot to run for Team USA at the world championships — a resolution to a widely-covered controversy in the running community.

items.headline: Arsenal Hit by Catastrophic International Injury Crisis — 9 Key Players Withdrawn Early
items.summary: Arsenal had 9+ first-team players return early from international duty injured, including Saka, Declan Rice, William Saliba, Jurrien Timber, Noni Madueke, Piero Hincapie, Leandro Trossard, and Martin Zubimendi. 8 of them are in Arsenal's top 10 players by minutes played this season. Timing is devastating with CL QF vs Sporting CP on April 7 and FA Cup 6th round vs Southampton on April 4.

items.headline: Gyokeres Hat-Trick + Penalty Sends Sweden to World Cup — Arsenal Striker Delivers
items.summary: Viktor Gyokeres scored a hat-trick to lead Sweden past Ukraine in the World Cup qualifying play-off semi-final, then scored the winning penalty against Poland in the final to seal qualification. The one bright international spot for Arsenal fans — Gyokeres returned healthy and in form.

items.headline: Bucks Officially Eliminated from Playoffs — First Time in a Decade
items.summary: The Milwaukee Bucks are officially out of the 2026 NBA playoffs, ending a decade-long postseason streak. The elimination was confirmed after Saturday's loss to the San Antonio Spurs. The Bucks finish 30-46. Giannis trade rumors are intensifying as the rebuild appears inevitable.

items.headline: Giannis Trade Path 'Much Clearer' as Bucks Season Collapses
items.summary: Fox Sports reports a storyline is forming that makes Giannis Antetokounmpo's departure from Milwaukee 'much clearer' — and one that doesn't paint him as a villain. With the Bucks missing playoffs for the first time in 10 years and sitting at 30-46, a rebuild and Giannis trade appear increasingly likely this summer.

items.headline: Luka Doncic Hamstring Injury — MRI Friday, Awards Eligibility at Risk
items.summary: Luka Doncic left the Lakers' blowout loss to OKC Thunder (139-96) in the third quarter with a hamstring injury. He'll undergo an MRI Friday. If he cannot return before the end of the regular season on April 12, he becomes ineligible for any postseason awards. Lakers are holding their breath.

items.headline: OKC Thunder Demolish Lakers 139-96 — Dominant Playoff Preview
items.summary: The Oklahoma City Thunder thrashed the Los Angeles Lakers 139-96 on Thursday in a dominant performance. The Thunder are rolling heading into the playoffs. The Lakers' night ended badly with Doncic's hamstring exit — Anthony Edwards also became ineligible for postseason awards.

items.headline: Houston Rockets Officially Clinch 2026 NBA Playoff Spot
items.summary: The Houston Rockets officially clinched their 2026 playoff berth, securing a top-6 seed that avoids the play-in tournament. A significant milestone for the franchise after a multi-year rebuilding project. They also beat the Bucks on April 1.

items.headline: Raiders Agree to Terms with Kirk Cousins
items.summary: The Las Vegas Raiders have agreed to terms with veteran QB Kirk Cousins, giving the team a bridge starter ahead of rookie Heisman winner Mendoza's development. Cousins will start while the Raiders manage their young QB's transition to the NFL.

items.headline: Aaron Rodgers Talking 'Regularly' with McCarthy — Decision Expected Before Draft
items.summary: Steelers ownership says Aaron Rodgers and head coach Mike McCarthy are talking regularly and a decision on Rodgers' 2026 status is expected before the NFL Draft (April 23-25 in Pittsburgh). Rodgers continues to keep the football world in suspense.

items.headline: Liverpool Putting 5 Players Up for Sale as Arne Slot Begins Major Rebuild
items.summary: Liverpool are reportedly putting five players on the market as manager Arne Slot begins what's being described as a significant squad overhaul this summer. The club is on strong financial footing after their 3-0 win vs Chelsea before the international break at their new stadium.

items.headline: Madueke Leaves Wembley in Knee Brace — Extent of Injury Unknown Ahead of CL Quarterfinal
items.summary: Noni Madueke was spotted leaving Wembley in a knee brace after being forced off in the first half of England's 1-1 draw with Uruguay. England boss Thomas Tuchel said 'I'm worried, of course — I think he was a difference-maker today.' The timing is dire with Arsenal's Champions League QF first leg vs Sporting CP on April 7.

items.headline: Saka, Rice, Saliba, Timber All Returned Early — Arteta Managing Injury List
items.summary: Bukayo Saka, Declan Rice, William Saliba, and Jurrien Timber all cut short their international duties and returned to Arsenal's training ground. Arteta's squad is stretched thin ahead of the season's biggest fixtures. Rival fans are criticizing Arsenal for 'protecting' players from international duty.

items.headline: FA Cup 6th Round vs Southampton (Apr 4), CL QF vs Sporting CP (Apr 7) — Critical Week Ahead
items.summary: Arsenal face one of the most important weeks of their season: FA Cup 6th round at Southampton on Saturday April 4, then Champions League quarterfinal first leg vs Sporting CP on Tuesday April 7. With the injury crisis at its worst, Arteta faces major selection headaches for both games.

items.headline: Arsenal's West Ham Fixture Moved to May 10 by Sky Sports
items.summary: Arsenal's trip to West Ham has been rescheduled to Sunday May 10 after being selected for UK broadcast by Sky Sports. The game was originally scheduled for an earlier date. Arsenal (29W-7D-3L) remain in Premier League title contention.

items.headline: Saliba Dishes Out Advice to Young Players — 'Agony' of International Break Injury Concerns
items.summary: William Saliba returned early from France duty and has been offering advice to younger Arsenal players dealing with the physical and psychological toll of the injury-plagued international break. Arteta's squad morale and rotation depth will be tested heavily this month.

items.headline: Bucks Officially Miss Playoffs for First Time in 10 Years — Season Over
items.summary: The Milwaukee Bucks' decade-long playoff streak is over. The team finishes 30-46 after a season marked by Giannis trade rumors, injuries, and inconsistency. Saturday's loss to the Spurs officially sealed their fate. The franchise now faces its most significant roster decisions in years.

items.headline: Giannis Trade Narrative Takes Shape — Path Out of Milwaukee 'Much Clearer'
items.summary: Fox Sports reports that a coherent trade narrative for Giannis Antetokounmpo is crystallizing — one that doesn't cast him as the villain. With the team missing playoffs and the front office facing a rebuild, the most likely outcome may be a sign-and-trade this summer. Giannis has been doing intense pregame workouts despite the team's situation.

items.headline: Celtics Dominate Bucks 147-129 — Jaylen Brown Drops 43 Points
items.summary: Boston Celtics (51-25) thrashed the Bucks 147-129 at Fiserv Forum. Jaylen Brown torched Milwaukee for 43 points on 58.6% shooting in 37 minutes. The Bucks' inability to defend elite scorers has been their Achilles heel all season.

items.headline: Bucks Beat Mavericks 123-99 — Ryan Rollins 24 Points, Snaps 4-Game Skid
items.summary: In their last win before elimination, the Bucks beat Dallas 123-99 with Ryan Rollins (24 pts, 7 reb, 9 ast) and Kyle Kuzma (20 pts) leading the way. Small moral victory but too little, too late for playoff hopes.

youtube.title: Google drops Gemma 4, Anthropic's SECRET AI Agent, Qwen 3.6 & MORE!
youtube.creator: Alex Finn
youtube.summary: Covers Google's Gemma 4 open model release (2B-31B, Apache 2.0), Anthropic's secret always-on Conway agent, Alibaba Qwen 3.6 Plus (free), and Z.ai GLM-5V Turbo with vision-to-code capabilities. A dense 24-hour AI roundup perfect for staying current on the open model and agent race.

youtube.title: AI News: Anthropic Went Crazy This Week!
youtube.creator: Matt Wolfe
youtube.summary: Matt Wolfe's weekly AI news roundup focusing on a big week from Anthropic. Covers recent Claude developments, tool updates, and major AI industry moves. Published about 1 week ago — still relevant as background context for this week's news.
